reasoning: The ambient space `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)` correctly represents ℝ^k. The hypothesis `¬ Set.Countable E` captures "E is uncountable". The set P is defined as {x | ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)}, which states that every neighborhood of x has uncountable intersection with E — this is exactly the standard definition of a condensation point (and it is equivalent to the "every open neighborhood" formulation since any neighborhood in 𝓝 x contains an open neighborhood and vice versa). The conclusion `Set.Countable (E \ P)` directly matches "at most countably many points of E are not in P". No hypotheses are added or dropped, and no types have been changed.
